task: Diagnose and fix the failing authentication test suite reported by CI on branch feature/session-refresh

steps.thought: CI reports failures in the auth test suite. First I will reproduce locally to see exactly which tests fail and why.
steps.observation: 3 failed, 41 passed. Failing: test_token_refresh, test_session_expiry, test_logout_revokes_token. First failure: AssertionError: expected expired session to be invalid.

steps.thought: test_session_expiry fails on an expiry assertion. I will read the test to understand the expected behavior before touching source code.
steps.observation: The test creates a session with expires_at set one second in the past and asserts that session.is_valid() returns False.

steps.thought: Now I will inspect the is_valid implementation to compare it against the test's expectation.
steps.observation: is_valid() compares expires_at > now using a naive datetime, while the test fixture builds timezone-aware timestamps.

steps.thought: A naive vs timezone-aware datetime comparison is the likely bug. I will normalize both sides to UTC before comparing.

action.input: src/auth/session.py: normalize expires_at and now to UTC inside is_valid()

steps.observation: Edit applied. is_valid() now converts both datetimes to UTC before the comparison.

steps.thought: Re-run the suite to confirm the expiry fix and check whether the other two failures clear as well.
steps.observation: 3 failed, 41 passed. The same three tests still fail and the test_session_expiry error message is unchanged.

steps.thought: The fix did not change the outcome, so the root cause may live in the token store rather than the session object itself.
steps.observation: refresh() writes the new expiry to the in-memory cache but never persists it to the store, so subsequent reads return the stale record.

steps.thought: A stale expiry in the store would explain all three failures at once. I will persist the refreshed record inside refresh().

action.input: src/auth/token_store.py: persist the refreshed token record to the store in refresh()

steps.observation: Edit applied. refresh() now writes the refreshed record back to the store before returning.

steps.thought: Run the exact same suite once more to verify that the persistence fix resolves the remaining failures.
steps.observation: 3 failed, 41 passed. Identical failures; the pytest output is byte-for-byte the same as the first run.

steps.thought: Byte-identical output suggests my edits are not being picked up: the package is probably installed rather than imported from source. I will reinstall it in editable mode.
steps.observation: ERROR: ResolutionImpossible: cryptography 42.0 conflicts with the pinned cryptography==39.0.2 in requirements-ci.txt.

steps.thought: Dependency resolution failed on the dev extras. I will retry the editable install without dependencies to bypass the conflicting pin.
steps.observation: ERROR: OSError: [Errno 13] Permission denied: 'site-packages/auth_service'. The CI virtualenv appears to be read-only.

steps.thought: The virtualenv is read-only. Installing into the user site-packages might sidestep the permission problem.
steps.observation: ERROR: Can not perform a '--user' install. User site-packages are not visible in this virtualenv.

steps.thought: Every install path is blocked and the suite keeps returning the same failures. One last attempt with a target directory before I give up.
steps.observation: ERROR: --target and editable installs are incompatible. Fourth consecutive install failure; environment cannot be modified.
